root_text.an6.62:1.2: ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā kosalesu cārikaṁ caramāno mahatā bhikkhusaṅghena saddhiṁ yena daṇḍakappakaṁ nāma kosalānaṁ nigamo tadavasari.
root_text.an6.62:1.3: Atha kho bhagavā maggā okkamma aññatarasmiṁ rukkhamūle paññatte āsane nisīdi.
root_text.an6.62:1.4: Te ca bhikkhū daṇḍakappakaṁ pavisiṁsu āvasathaṁ pariyesituṁ.
root_text.an6.62:2.1: Atha kho āyasmā ānando sambahulehi bhikkhūhi saddhiṁ yena aciravatī nadī tenupasaṅkami gattāni parisiñcituṁ.
root_text.an6.62:2.2: Aciravatiyā nadiyā gattāni parisiñcitvā paccuttaritvā ekacīvaro aṭṭhāsi gattāni pubbāpayamāno.
root_text.an6.62:2.3: Atha kho aññataro bhikkhu yenāyasmā ānando tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā āyasmantaṁ ānandaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an6.62:2.4: “kiṁ nu kho, āvuso ānanda, sabbaṁ cetaso samannāharitvā nu kho devadatto bhagavatā byākato:
root_text.an6.62:2.5: ‘āpāyiko devadatto nerayiko kappaṭṭho atekiccho’ti, udāhu kenacideva pariyāyenāti?
root_text.an6.62:2.6: Evaṁ kho panetaṁ, āvuso, bhagavatā byākatan”ti.
root_text.an6.62:3.1: Atha kho āyasmā ānando yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho āyasmā ānando bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an6.62:3.2: “idhāhaṁ, bhante, sambahulehi bhikkhūhi saddhiṁ yena aciravatī nadī tenupasaṅkamiṁ gattāni parisiñcituṁ.
root_text.an6.62:3.3: Aciravatiyā nadiyā gattāni parisiñcitvā paccuttaritvā ekacīvaro aṭṭhāsiṁ gattāni pubbāpayamāno.
root_text.an6.62:3.4: Atha kho, bhante, aññataro bhikkhu yenāhaṁ tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā maṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an6.62:3.5: ‘kiṁ nu kho, āvuso ānanda, sabbaṁ cetaso samannāharitvā nu kho devadatto bhagavatā byākato—
root_text.an6.62:3.6: āpāyiko devadatto nerayiko kappaṭṭho atekicchoti, udāhu kenacideva pariyāyenā’ti?
root_text.an6.62:3.7: Evaṁ vutte, ahaṁ, bhante, taṁ bhikkhuṁ etadavocaṁ:
root_text.an6.62:4.1: “So vā kho, ānanda, bhikkhu navo bhavissati acirapabbajito, thero vā pana bālo abyatto.
root_text.an6.62:4.2: Kathañhi nāma yaṁ mayā ekaṁsena byākataṁ tattha dvejjhaṁ āpajjissati.
root_text.an6.62:4.3: Nāhaṁ, ānanda, aññaṁ ekapuggalampi samanupassāmi, yo evaṁ mayā sabbaṁ cetaso samannāharitvā byākato, yathayidaṁ devadatto.
root_text.an6.62:4.4: Yāvakīvañcāhaṁ, ānanda, devadattassa vālaggakoṭinittudanamattampi sukkadhammaṁ addasaṁ;
root_text.an6.62:4.7: Yato ca kho ahaṁ, ānanda, devadattassa vālaggakoṭinittudanamattampi sukkadhammaṁ na addasaṁ;
root_text.an6.62:5.1: Seyyathāpi, ānanda, gūthakūpo sādhikaporiso pūro gūthassa samatittiko.
root_text.an6.62:5.3: Tassa kocideva puriso uppajjeyya atthakāmo hitakāmo yogakkhemakāmo tamhā gūthakūpā uddharitukāmo.
root_text.an6.62:5.4: So taṁ gūthakūpaṁ samantānuparigacchanto neva passeyya tassa purisassa vālaggakoṭinittudanamattampi gūthena amakkhitaṁ, yattha taṁ gahetvā uddhareyya.
root_text.an6.62:5.5: Evamevaṁ kho ahaṁ, ānanda, yato devadattassa vālaggakoṭinittudanamattampi sukkadhammaṁ na addasaṁ;
root_text.an6.62:5.8: Sace tumhe, ānanda, suṇeyyātha tathāgatassa purisindriyañāṇāni vibhajissāmī”ti?
root_text.an6.62:6.2: yaṁ bhagavā purisindriyañāṇāni vibhajeyya. Bhagavato sutvā bhikkhū dhāressantī”ti.
root_text.an6.62:6.4: “Evaṁ, bhante”ti kho āyasmā ānando bhagavato paccassosi.
root_text.an6.62:7.1: “Idhāhaṁ, ānanda, ekaccaṁ puggalaṁ evaṁ cetasā ceto paricca pajānāmi:
root_text.an6.62:7.2: ‘imassa kho puggalassa vijjamānā kusalāpi dhammā akusalāpi dhammā’ti.
root_text.an6.62:7.3: Tamenaṁ aparena samayena evaṁ cetasā ceto paricca pajānāmi:
root_text.an6.62:7.4: ‘imassa kho puggalassa kusalā dhammā antarahitā, akusalā dhammā sammukhībhūtā.
root_text.an6.62:7.5: Atthi ca khvassa kusalamūlaṁ asamucchinnaṁ, tamhā tassa kusalā kusalaṁ pātubhavissati.
root_text.an6.62:7.7: Seyyathāpi, ānanda, bījāni akhaṇḍāni apūtīni avātātapahatāni sāradāni sukhasayitāni sukhette suparikammakatāya bhūmiyā nikkhittāni.
root_text.an6.62:7.8: Jāneyyāsi tvaṁ, ānanda, imāni bījāni vuddhiṁ virūḷhiṁ vepullaṁ āpajjissantī”ti?
root_text.an6.62:7.10: “Evamevaṁ kho ahaṁ, ānanda, idhekaccaṁ puggalaṁ evaṁ cetasā ceto paricca pajānāmi:
root_text.an6.62:7.11: ‘imassa kho puggalassa vijjamānā kusalāpi dhammā akusalāpi dhammā’ti.
root_text.an6.62:7.12: Tamenaṁ aparena samayena evaṁ cetasā ceto paricca pajānāmi:
root_text.an6.62:7.13: ‘imassa kho puggalassa kusalā dhammā antarahitā, akusalā dhammā sammukhībhūtā.
root_text.an6.62:7.14: Atthi ca khvassa kusalamūlaṁ asamucchinnaṁ, tamhā tassa kusalā kusalaṁ pātubhavissati.
root_text.an6.62:7.16: Evampi kho, ānanda, tathāgatassa purisapuggalo cetasā ceto paricca vidito hoti.
root_text.an6.62:7.17: Evampi kho, ānanda, tathāgatassa purisindriyañāṇaṁ cetasā ceto paricca viditaṁ hoti.
root_text.an6.62:7.18: Evampi kho, ānanda, tathāgatassa āyatiṁ dhammasamuppādo cetasā ceto paricca vidito hoti.
root_text.an6.62:8.1: Idha panāhaṁ, ānanda, ekaccaṁ puggalaṁ evaṁ cetasā ceto paricca pajānāmi:
root_text.an6.62:8.2: ‘imassa kho puggalassa vijjamānā kusalāpi dhammā akusalāpi dhammā’ti.
root_text.an6.62:8.3: Tamenaṁ aparena samayena evaṁ cetasā ceto paricca pajānāmi:
root_text.an6.62:8.4: ‘imassa kho puggalassa akusalā dhammā antarahitā, kusalā dhammā sammukhībhūtā.
root_text.an6.62:8.5: Atthi ca khvassa akusalamūlaṁ asamucchinnaṁ, tamhā tassa akusalā akusalaṁ pātubhavissati.
root_text.an6.62:8.7: Seyyathāpi, ānanda, bījāni akhaṇḍāni apūtīni avātātapahatāni sāradāni sukhasayitāni puthusilāya nikkhittāni.
root_text.an6.62:8.8: Jāneyyāsi tvaṁ, ānanda, nayimāni bījāni vuddhiṁ virūḷhiṁ vepullaṁ āpajjissantī”ti?
root_text.an6.62:8.10: “Evamevaṁ kho ahaṁ, ānanda, idhekaccaṁ puggalaṁ evaṁ cetasā ceto paricca pajānāmi:
root_text.an6.62:8.11: ‘imassa kho puggalassa vijjamānā kusalāpi dhammā akusalāpi dhammā’ti.
root_text.an6.62:8.12: Tamenaṁ aparena samayena evaṁ cetasā ceto paricca pajānāmi:
root_text.an6.62:8.13: ‘imassa kho puggalassa akusalā dhammā antarahitā, kusalā dhammā sammukhībhūtā.
root_text.an6.62:8.14: Atthi ca khvassa akusalamūlaṁ asamucchinnaṁ, tamhā tassa akusalā akusalaṁ pātubhavissati.
root_text.an6.62:8.16: Evampi kho, ānanda, tathāgatassa purisapuggalo cetasā ceto paricca vidito hoti.
root_text.an6.62:8.17: Evampi kho, ānanda, tathāgatassa purisindriyañāṇaṁ cetasā ceto paricca viditaṁ hoti.
root_text.an6.62:8.18: Evampi kho, ānanda, tathāgatassa āyatiṁ dhammasamuppādo cetasā ceto paricca vidito hoti.
root_text.an6.62:9.1: Idha panāhaṁ, ānanda, ekaccaṁ puggalaṁ evaṁ cetasā ceto paricca pajānāmi:
root_text.an6.62:9.2: ‘imassa kho puggalassa vijjamānā kusalāpi dhammā akusalāpi dhammā’ti.
root_text.an6.62:9.3: Tamenaṁ aparena samayena evaṁ cetasā ceto paricca pajānāmi:
root_text.an6.62:9.4: ‘natthi imassa puggalassa vālaggakoṭinittudanamattopi sukko dhammo, samannāgatoyaṁ puggalo ekantakāḷakehi akusalehi dhammehi, kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā apāyaṁ duggatiṁ vinipātaṁ nirayaṁ upapajjissatī’ti.
root_text.an6.62:9.5: Seyyathāpi, ānanda, bījāni khaṇḍāni pūtīni vātātapahatāni sukhette suparikammakatāya bhūmiyā nikkhittāni.
root_text.an6.62:9.6: Jāneyyāsi tvaṁ, ānanda, nayimāni bījāni vuddhiṁ virūḷhiṁ vepullaṁ āpajjissantī”ti?
root_text.an6.62:9.8: “Evamevaṁ kho ahaṁ, ānanda, idhekaccaṁ puggalaṁ evaṁ cetasā ceto paricca pajānāmi:
root_text.an6.62:9.9: ‘imassa kho puggalassa vijjamānā kusalāpi dhammā akusalāpi dhammā’ti.
root_text.an6.62:9.10: Tamenaṁ aparena samayena evaṁ cetasā ceto paricca pajānāmi:
root_text.an6.62:9.11: ‘natthi imassa puggalassa vālaggakoṭinittudanamattopi sukko dhammo, samannāgatoyaṁ puggalo ekantakāḷakehi akusalehi dhammehi, kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā apāyaṁ duggatiṁ vinipātaṁ nirayaṁ upapajjissatī’ti.
root_text.an6.62:9.12: Evampi kho, ānanda, tathāgatassa purisapuggalo cetasā ceto paricca vidito hoti.
root_text.an6.62:9.13: Evampi kho, ānanda, tathāgatassa purisindriyañāṇaṁ cetasā ceto paricca viditaṁ hoti.
root_text.an6.62:9.14: Evampi kho, ānanda, tathāgatassa āyatiṁ dhammasamuppādo cetasā ceto paricca vidito hotī”ti.
root_text.an6.62:10.1: Evaṁ vutte, āyasmā ānando bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an6.62:10.2: “sakkā nu kho, bhante, imesaṁ tiṇṇaṁ puggalānaṁ aparepi tayo puggalā sappaṭibhāgā paññāpetun”ti?
root_text.an6.62:10.4: “idhāhaṁ, ānanda, ekaccaṁ puggalaṁ evaṁ cetasā ceto paricca pajānāmi:
root_text.an6.62:10.5: ‘imassa kho puggalassa vijjamānā kusalāpi dhammā akusalāpi dhammā’ti.
root_text.an6.62:10.6: Tamenaṁ aparena samayena evaṁ cetasā ceto paricca pajānāmi:
root_text.an6.62:10.7: ‘imassa kho puggalassa kusalā dhammā antarahitā, akusalā dhammā sammukhībhūtā.
root_text.an6.62:10.8: Atthi ca khvassa kusalamūlaṁ asamucchinnaṁ, tampi sabbena sabbaṁ samugghātaṁ gacchati.
root_text.an6.62:10.10: Seyyathāpi, ānanda, aṅgārāni ādittāni sampajjalitāni sajotibhūtāni puthusilāya nikkhittāni.
root_text.an6.62:10.11: Jāneyyāsi tvaṁ, ānanda, nayimāni aṅgārāni vuddhiṁ virūḷhiṁ vepullaṁ āpajjissantī”ti?
root_text.an6.62:10.13: “Seyyathāpi vā pana, ānanda, sāyanhasamayaṁ sūriye ogacchante, jāneyyāsi tvaṁ, ānanda, āloko antaradhāyissati andhakāro pātubhavissatī”ti?
root_text.an6.62:10.15: “Seyyathāpi vā, panānanda, abhido addharattaṁ bhattakālasamaye, jāneyyāsi tvaṁ, ānanda, āloko antarahito andhakāro pātubhūto”ti?
root_text.an6.62:10.17: “Evamevaṁ kho ahaṁ, ānanda, idhekaccaṁ puggalaṁ evaṁ cetasā ceto paricca pajānāmi:
root_text.an6.62:10.18: ‘imassa kho puggalassa vijjamānā kusalāpi dhammā akusalāpi dhammā’ti.
root_text.an6.62:10.19: Tamenaṁ aparena samayena evaṁ cetasā ceto paricca pajānāmi:
root_text.an6.62:10.20: ‘imassa kho puggalassa kusalā dhammā antarahitā, akusalā dhammā sammukhībhūtā.
root_text.an6.62:10.21: Atthi ca khvassa kusalamūlaṁ asamucchinnaṁ, tampi sabbena sabbaṁ samugghātaṁ gacchati.
root_text.an6.62:10.23: Evampi kho, ānanda, tathāgatassa purisapuggalo cetasā ceto paricca vidito hoti.
root_text.an6.62:10.24: Evampi kho, ānanda, tathāgatassa purisindriyañāṇaṁ cetasā ceto paricca viditaṁ hoti.
root_text.an6.62:10.25: Evampi kho, ānanda, tathāgatassa āyatiṁ dhammasamuppādo cetasā ceto paricca vidito hoti.
root_text.an6.62:11.1: Idha panāhaṁ, ānanda, ekaccaṁ puggalaṁ evaṁ cetasā ceto paricca pajānāmi:
root_text.an6.62:11.2: ‘imassa kho puggalassa vijjamānā kusalāpi dhammā akusalāpi dhammā’ti.
root_text.an6.62:11.3: Tamenaṁ aparena samayena evaṁ cetasā ceto paricca pajānāmi:
root_text.an6.62:11.4: ‘imassa kho puggalassa akusalā dhammā antarahitā, kusalā dhammā sammukhībhūtā.
root_text.an6.62:11.5: Atthi ca khvassa akusalamūlaṁ asamucchinnaṁ, tampi sabbena sabbaṁ samugghātaṁ gacchati.
root_text.an6.62:11.7: Seyyathāpi, ānanda, aṅgārāni ādittāni sampajjalitāni sajotibhūtāni sukkhe tiṇapuñje vā kaṭṭhapuñje vā nikkhittāni.
root_text.an6.62:11.8: Jāneyyāsi tvaṁ, ānanda, imāni aṅgārāni vuḍḍhiṁ virūḷhiṁ vepullaṁ āpajjissantī”ti?
root_text.an6.62:11.10: “Seyyathāpi vā panānanda, rattiyā paccūsasamayaṁ sūriye uggacchante, jāneyyāsi tvaṁ, ānanda, andhakāro antaradhāyissati, āloko pātubhavissatī”ti?
root_text.an6.62:11.12: “Seyyathāpi vā panānanda, abhido majjhanhike bhattakālasamaye, jāneyyāsi tvaṁ, ānanda, andhakāro antarahito āloko pātubhūto”ti?
root_text.an6.62:11.14: “Evamevaṁ kho ahaṁ, ānanda, idhekaccaṁ puggalaṁ evaṁ cetasā ceto paricca pajānāmi:
root_text.an6.62:11.15: ‘imassa kho puggalassa vijjamānā kusalāpi dhammā akusalāpi dhammā’ti.
root_text.an6.62:11.16: Tamenaṁ aparena samayena evaṁ cetasā ceto paricca pajānāmi:
root_text.an6.62:11.17: ‘imassa kho puggalassa akusalā dhammā antarahitā, kusalā dhammā sammukhībhūtā.
root_text.an6.62:11.18: Atthi ca khvassa akusalamūlaṁ asamucchinnaṁ, tampi sabbena sabbaṁ samugghātaṁ gacchati.
root_text.an6.62:11.20: Evampi kho, ānanda, tathāgatassa purisapuggalo cetasā ceto paricca vidito hoti.
root_text.an6.62:11.21: Evampi kho, ānanda, tathāgatassa purisindriyañāṇaṁ cetasā ceto paricca viditaṁ hoti.
root_text.an6.62:11.22: Evampi kho, ānanda, tathāgatassa āyatiṁ dhammasamuppādo cetasā ceto paricca vidito hoti.
root_text.an6.62:12.1: Idha panāhaṁ, ānanda, ekaccaṁ puggalaṁ cetasā ceto paricca pajānāmi:
root_text.an6.62:12.2: ‘imassa kho puggalassa vijjamānā kusalāpi dhammā akusalāpi dhammā’ti.
root_text.an6.62:12.3: Tamenaṁ aparena samayena evaṁ cetasā ceto paricca pajānāmi:
root_text.an6.62:12.4: ‘natthi imassa puggalassa vālaggakoṭinittudanamattopi akusalo dhammo, samannāgatoyaṁ puggalo ekantasukkehi anavajjehi dhammehi, diṭṭheva dhamme parinibbāyissatī’ti.
root_text.an6.62:12.5: Seyyathāpi, ānanda, aṅgārāni sītāni nibbutāni sukkhe tiṇapuñje vā kaṭṭhapuñje vā nikkhittāni.
root_text.an6.62:12.6: Jāneyyāsi tvaṁ, ānanda, nayimāni aṅgārāni vuḍḍhiṁ virūḷhiṁ vepullaṁ āpajjissantī”ti?
root_text.an6.62:12.8: “Evamevaṁ kho ahaṁ, ānanda, idhekaccaṁ puggalaṁ evaṁ cetasā ceto paricca pajānāmi:
root_text.an6.62:12.9: ‘imassa kho puggalassa vijjamānā kusalāpi dhammā akusalāpi dhammā’ti.
root_text.an6.62:12.10: Tamenaṁ aparena samayena evaṁ cetasā ceto paricca pajānāmi:
root_text.an6.62:12.11: ‘natthi imassa puggalassa vālaggakoṭinittudanamattopi akusalo dhammo, samannāgatoyaṁ puggalo ekantasukkehi anavajjehi dhammehi, diṭṭheva dhamme parinibbāyissatī’ti.
root_text.an6.62:12.12: Evampi kho, ānanda, tathāgatassa purisapuggalo cetasā ceto paricca vidito hoti.
root_text.an6.62:12.13: Evampi kho, ānanda, tathāgatassa purisindriyañāṇaṁ cetasā ceto paricca viditaṁ hoti.
root_text.an6.62:12.14: Evampi kho, ānanda, tathāgatassa āyatiṁ dhammasamuppādo cetasā ceto paricca vidito hoti.
root_text.an6.62:13.1: Tatrānanda, ye te purimā tayo puggalā tesaṁ tiṇṇaṁ puggalānaṁ eko aparihānadhammo, eko parihānadhammo, eko āpāyiko nerayiko.
root_text.an6.62:13.2: Tatrānanda, yeme pacchimā tayo puggalā imesaṁ tiṇṇaṁ puggalānaṁ eko parihānadhammo, eko aparihānadhammo, eko parinibbānadhammo”ti.

translation_text.an6.62:0.3: Knowledge of the Faculties of Persons
translation_text.an6.62:1.2: At one time the Buddha was wandering in the land of the Kosalans together with a large Saṅgha of mendicants when he arrived at a town of the Kosalans named Daṇḍakappaka.
translation_text.an6.62:1.3: The Buddha left the road and sat at the root of a tree on the seat spread out.
translation_text.an6.62:1.4: The mendicants entered Daṇḍakappaka to look for a guest house.
translation_text.an6.62:2.1: Then Venerable Ānanda together with several mendicants went to the Aciravatī River to bathe.
translation_text.an6.62:2.2: When he had bathed and emerged from the water he stood in one robe drying his limbs.
translation_text.an6.62:2.3: Then a certain mendicant went up to Venerable Ānanda, and said to him,
translation_text.an6.62:2.4: “Reverend Ānanda, when the Buddha declared that Devadatta was
translation_text.an6.62:2.5: going to a place of loss, to hell, there to remain for an eon, irredeemable, did he do so after wholeheartedly deliberating, or was this just a way of speaking?”
translation_text.an6.62:2.6: “You’re right, reverend, that’s how the Buddha declared it.”
translation_text.an6.62:3.1: Then Venerable Ānanda went up to the Buddha, bowed, sat down to one side, and told him what had happened.
translation_text.an6.62:4.1: “Ānanda, that mendicant must be junior, recently gone forth, or else a foolish, incompetent senior mendicant.
translation_text.an6.62:4.2: How on earth can he take something that I have declared categorically to be ambiguous?
translation_text.an6.62:4.3: I do not see a single other individual about whom I have given such whole-hearted deliberation before making a declaration as I did in the case of Devadatta.
translation_text.an6.62:4.4: As long as I saw even a fraction of a hair’s tip of goodness in Devadatta
translation_text.an6.62:4.5: I did not declare that he was
translation_text.an6.62:4.6: going to a place of loss, to hell, there to remain for an eon, irredeemable.
translation_text.an6.62:4.7: But when I saw that there was not even a fraction of a hair’s tip of goodness in Devadatta
translation_text.an6.62:4.9: going to a place of loss, to hell, there to remain for an eon, irredeemable.
translation_text.an6.62:5.1: Suppose there was a sewer deeper than a man’s height, full to the brim with feces,
translation_text.an6.62:5.2: and someone was sunk into it over their head.
translation_text.an6.62:5.3: Then along comes a person who wants to help make them safe, who wants to lift them out of that sewer.
translation_text.an6.62:5.4: But circling all around the sewer they couldn’t see even a fraction of a hair’s tip of that person that was not smeared with feces.
translation_text.an6.62:5.5: In the same way, when I saw that there was not even a fraction of a hair’s tip of goodness in Devadatta
translation_text.an6.62:5.7: going to a place of loss, to hell, there to remain for an eon, irredeemable.
translation_text.an6.62:5.8: If you would all listen, Ānanda, I will analyze the Realized One’s knowledges of the faculties of persons.”
translation_text.an6.62:6.1: “Now is the time, Blessed One! Now is the time, Holy One!
translation_text.an6.62:6.2: Let the Buddha analyze the faculties of persons. The mendicants will listen and remember it.”
translation_text.an6.62:6.3: “Well then, Ānanda, listen and apply your mind well, I will speak.”
translation_text.an6.62:7.1: “Ānanda, when I’ve comprehended the mind of an individual, I understand:
translation_text.an6.62:7.2: ‘Both skillful and unskillful qualities are found in this individual.’
translation_text.an6.62:7.3: After some time I encompass their mind and understand:
translation_text.an6.62:7.4: ‘The skillful qualities of this individual have vanished, but the unskillful qualities are still present.
translation_text.an6.62:7.5: Nevertheless, their skillful root is unbroken, and from that the skillful will appear.
translation_text.an6.62:7.6: So this individual is not liable to decline in the future.’
translation_text.an6.62:7.7: Suppose some seeds were intact, unspoiled, not weather-damaged, fertile, and well-kept. They’re sown in a well-prepared, productive field.
translation_text.an6.62:7.8: Wouldn’t you know that those seeds would grow, increase, and mature?”
translation_text.an6.62:7.10: “In the same way, when I’ve comprehended the mind of an individual, I understand …
translation_text.an6.62:7.15: This individual is not liable to decline in the future …
translation_text.an6.62:7.16: This is how another individual is known to the Realized One by encompassing their mind.
translation_text.an6.62:7.17: And this is how the Realized One knows a person’s faculties by encompassing their mind.
translation_text.an6.62:7.18: And this is how the Realized One knows the future origination of a person’s qualities by encompassing their mind.
translation_text.an6.62:8.1: When I’ve comprehended the mind of an individual, I understand:
translation_text.an6.62:8.2: ‘Both skillful and unskillful qualities are found in this individual.’
translation_text.an6.62:8.3: After some time I encompass their mind and understand:
translation_text.an6.62:8.4: ‘The unskillful qualities of this individual have vanished, but the skillful qualities are still present.
translation_text.an6.62:8.5: Nevertheless, their unskillful root is unbroken, and from that the unskillful will appear.
translation_text.an6.62:8.6: So this individual is still liable to decline in the future.’
translation_text.an6.62:8.7: Suppose some seeds were intact, unspoiled, not weather-damaged, fertile, and well-kept. And they were sown on a broad rock.
translation_text.an6.62:8.8: Wouldn’t you know that those seeds would not grow, increase, and mature?”
translation_text.an6.62:8.10: “In the same way, when I’ve comprehended the mind of an individual, I understand …
translation_text.an6.62:8.15: This individual is still liable to decline in the future …
translation_text.an6.62:8.16: This is how another individual is known to the Realized One …
translation_text.an6.62:9.1: When I’ve comprehended the mind of an individual, I understand:
translation_text.an6.62:9.2: ‘Both skillful and unskillful qualities are found in this individual.’
translation_text.an6.62:9.3: After some time I encompass their mind and understand:
translation_text.an6.62:9.4: ‘This individual has not even a fraction of a hair’s tip of goodness. They have exclusively dark, unskillful qualities. When their body breaks up, after death, they will be reborn in a place of loss, a bad place, the underworld, hell.’
translation_text.an6.62:9.5: Suppose some seeds were broken, spoiled, weather-damaged. They’re sown in a well-prepared, productive field.
translation_text.an6.62:9.6: Wouldn’t you know that those seeds would not grow, increase, and mature?”
translation_text.an6.62:9.8: “In the same way, when I’ve comprehended the mind of an individual, I understand …
translation_text.an6.62:9.11: ‘This individual has not even a fraction of a hair’s tip of goodness. They have exclusively dark, unskillful qualities. When their body breaks up, after death, they will be reborn in a place of loss, a bad place, the underworld, hell.’ …”
translation_text.an6.62:10.1: When he said this, Venerable Ānanda said to the Buddha,
translation_text.an6.62:10.2: “Sir, can you describe three other individuals who are counterparts of these three?”
translation_text.an6.62:10.4: “Ānanda, when I’ve comprehended the mind of an individual, I understand:
translation_text.an6.62:10.5: ‘Both skillful and unskillful qualities are found in this individual.’
translation_text.an6.62:10.6: After some time I encompass their mind and understand:
translation_text.an6.62:10.7: ‘The skillful qualities of this individual have vanished, but the unskillful qualities are still present.
translation_text.an6.62:10.8: Nevertheless, their skillful root is unbroken, but it’s about to be totally destroyed.
translation_text.an6.62:10.9: So this individual is still liable to decline in the future.’
translation_text.an6.62:10.10: Suppose that there were some burning coals, blazing and glowing. And they were placed on a broad rock.
translation_text.an6.62:10.11: Wouldn’t you know that those coals would not grow, increase, and spread?”
translation_text.an6.62:10.13: “Or suppose it was the late afternoon and the sun was going down. Wouldn’t you know that the light was about to vanish and darkness appear?”
translation_text.an6.62:10.15: “Or suppose that it’s nearly time for the midnight meal. Wouldn’t you know that the light had vanished and the darkness appeared?”
translation_text.an6.62:10.17: “In the same way, when I’ve comprehended the mind of an individual, I understand …
translation_text.an6.62:10.22: This individual is still liable to decline in the future …
translation_text.an6.62:11.1: When I’ve comprehended the mind of an individual, I understand:
translation_text.an6.62:11.2: ‘Both skillful and unskillful qualities are found in this individual.’
translation_text.an6.62:11.3: After some time I encompass their mind and understand:
translation_text.an6.62:11.4: ‘The unskillful qualities of this individual have vanished, but the skillful qualities are still present.
translation_text.an6.62:11.5: Nevertheless, their unskillful root is unbroken, but it’s about to be totally destroyed.
translation_text.an6.62:11.6: So this individual is not liable to decline in the future.’
translation_text.an6.62:11.7: Suppose that there were some burning coals, blazing and glowing. They were placed on a pile of grass or timber.
translation_text.an6.62:11.8: Wouldn’t you know that those coals would grow, increase, and spread?”
translation_text.an6.62:11.10: “Suppose it’s the crack of dawn and the sun is rising. Wouldn’t you know that the dark will vanish and the light appear?”
translation_text.an6.62:11.12: “Or suppose that it’s nearly time for the midday meal. Wouldn’t you know that the dark had vanished and the light appeared?”
translation_text.an6.62:11.14: “In the same way, when I’ve comprehended the mind of an individual, I understand …
translation_text.an6.62:11.19: This individual is not liable to decline in the future …
translation_text.an6.62:12.1: When I’ve comprehended the mind of an individual, I understand:
translation_text.an6.62:12.2: ‘Both skillful and unskillful qualities are found in this individual.’
translation_text.an6.62:12.3: After some time I encompass their mind and understand:
translation_text.an6.62:12.4: ‘This individual has not even a fraction of a hair’s tip of unskillful qualities. They have exclusively bright, blameless qualities. They will become extinguished in this very life.’
translation_text.an6.62:12.5: Suppose that there were some cool, quenched coals. They were placed on a pile of grass or timber.
translation_text.an6.62:12.6: Wouldn’t you know that those coals would not grow, increase, and spread?”
translation_text.an6.62:12.8: “In the same way, when I’ve comprehended the mind of an individual, I understand …
translation_text.an6.62:12.11: ‘This individual has not even a fraction of a hair’s tip of unskillful qualities. They have exclusively bright, blameless qualities. They will become extinguished in this very life.’
translation_text.an6.62:12.12: This is how another individual is known to the Realized One by encompassing their mind.
translation_text.an6.62:12.13: And this is how the Realized One knows a person’s faculties by encompassing their mind.
translation_text.an6.62:12.14: And this is how the Realized One knows the future origination of a person’s qualities by encompassing their mind.
translation_text.an6.62:13.1: And so, Ānanda, of the first three individuals one is not liable to decline, one is liable to decline, and one is bound for a place of loss, hell.
translation_text.an6.62:13.2: And of the second three individuals, one is liable to decline, one is not liable to decline, and one is bound to become extinguished.”
